name: JEPA Artifact Pack Example
title: JEPA Artifact Pack
description: Non-normative example demonstrating how OKPF MAY package model metadata, training provenance, evaluation reports, and model cards for a JEPA-family architecture. No model weights are included. All numeric values are illustrative placeholders.

license.details: Example content only. No model weights are included. See docs/model_card.md for attribution.

usage_policy.notes: This is a non-normative example pack. All content is illustrative. No real trained model is referenced or distributed.

artifacts.title: JEPA Model Card
artifacts.description: Human-readable model card: intended use, out-of-scope uses, limitations, and attribution.

artifacts.title: Known Limitations
artifacts.description: Structured limitations summary for architecture, packaging, provenance, and evaluation scope.

artifacts.title: Representation Metadata
artifacts.description: Architecture summary and representation space metadata. No model weights are included or referenced.

artifacts.title: Training Run Provenance
artifacts.description: Placeholder training provenance record: dataset pointer, compute summary, hyperparameters, framework.

artifacts.title: JEPA Evaluation Report
artifacts.description: Placeholder benchmark results and evaluation methodology summary.

expert_notes.rationale: Demonstrates how OKPF can package model metadata, provenance, and evaluation artifacts without defining model weights, inference semantics, or training pipelines. OKPF is the packaging layer; the ML framework remains independent.
assumptions: OKPF does not define neural architectures, inference engines, training pipelines, or weight serialization formats.
All numeric values (benchmark scores, parameter counts, compute estimates) are illustrative placeholders, not results from a real training run.
Architecture references (ViT-H/16, I-JEPA) are used for illustration. No affiliation with Meta AI or the I-JEPA authors is implied.
limitations: No model weights are included. This is a Native Mode pack: documentation, provenance, and evaluation artifacts only.
SHA-256 hashes are omitted from artifact entries in this example. A production pack SHOULD declare per-artifact sha256 values.
OKPF Core validation checks pack structure and integrity hashes. It does not verify the factual accuracy of provenance records or benchmark claims.
expert_notes.decision_basis: Use Native Mode with extension metadata for JEPA-specific fields. All JEPA support lives in artifacts and the extensions block — no OKPF Core schema changes.
expert_notes.review_notes: This is a non-normative example. It does not establish normative conventions for ML model packaging in OKPF Core.

jepa.reference: Assran et al., 2023 — Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture
jepa.notes: Extension metadata is advisory. Unknown extension keys MUST NOT invalidate a core-valid OKPF pack.
